---
title: "LRPITT28: Site-adjusted ITT effect of group assignment on letter-sound knowledge (L)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by an LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8).
:::

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPITT28 is the **site-adjusted** ITT analysis for letter-sound knowledge (YARC-LSK) (`yarclet`) — the site-robustness companion to LRPITT07. It uses the uniform LRPITT spec plus a linear adjustment for study site (`area`: 1 = North, 2 = South; two sites, 30 / 24 children). Unlike the SES-adjusted LRPITT13b, `area` is **fully observed**, so there is no complete-case dropping: the fit stays on all 54 children and its $\tau$ is directly comparable to the unadjusted LRPITT07 (no matched comparator needed). The estimand is the total effect of randomised assignment $G$ on $L_{\text{post}}$ (RCT phase only).

::: {.callout-note title="Identification and sign convention"}

Under the locked causal DAG, $G$ is a randomised root, so the ITT effect $\tau$ is
identified by the **empty adjustment set**. The own baseline, linear age, and the
site term are **precision / robustness terms only** — they sharpen $\tau$ and probe
its stability, but are not what licenses the causal interpretation.
**Sign convention:** $G = 1$ is the intervention arm, so a **positive** $\tau$ means
the intervention raises the outcome.

:::

## Model

$$
\eta_i = \alpha + \tau\, G_i
       + \gamma_{\text{own}}\, \text{logit}_*(L_{\mathrm{pre},i}, N)
       + \gamma_A\, A_{\text{std},i} + \gamma_{\text{area}}\, z(\text{area}_i),
$$

with Beta-Binomial likelihood $L_{\text{post},i} \sim \text{BetaBinomial}(N, \mu_i\kappa, (1-\mu_i)\kappa)$,
$\mu_i = \sigma(\eta_i)$, $N = 32$. The site term enters as a linear $\gamma_{\text{area}}$
(the same weak $\text{Normal}(0, 0.3)$ prior as cross-baseline couplings); HSGPs are
off by default. Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_itt.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
